Synthetic multi-series monthly retail sales

Source: R/data.R
milt_retail.Rd

Monthly sales figures (synthetic) for five product categories from January 2010 to December 2022 (156 months each). Each series exhibits a linear trend, monthly seasonality, and Gaussian noise, making this dataset suitable for multi-series forecasting demonstrations.

Usage

milt_retail

Format

A tibble::tibble() with 780 rows (5 categories \(\times\) 156 months) and 3 columns:

date

Date. First day of each month.

category

character. One of "Electronics", "Clothing", "Food", "Furniture", "Toys".

sales

numeric. Monthly sales in arbitrary units (non-negative).

Source

Synthetically generated by data-raw/prepare_datasets.R.

See also

milt_series(), milt_air, milt_energy

Other datasets: milt_air, milt_energy

Examples

# \donttest{
data(milt_retail)
elec <- milt_retail[milt_retail$category == "Electronics", ]
s <- milt_series(elec, time_col = "date", value_cols = "sales",
                 frequency = "monthly")
s
#> # A MiltSeries: 156 x 1 [monthly]
#> # Time range : 2010 Jan — 2022 Dec
#> # Components : sales
#> # Gaps       : none
#> # A tibble: 6 × 3
#>   date       category    sales
#>   <date>     <chr>       <dbl>
#> 1 2010-01-01 Electronics 1132.
#> 2 2010-02-01 Electronics 1073.
#> 3 2010-03-01 Electronics 1104.
#> 4 2010-04-01 Electronics 1087.
#> 5 2010-05-01 Electronics 1037.
#> 6 2010-06-01 Electronics  974.
#> # … with 150 more rows
# }
